expectations.text: Classifies the CV screening/ranking tool as HIGH-RISK under Annex III point 4(a) (employment: recruitment or selection, incl. placing targeted job ads, analysing and filtering applications, evaluating candidates)
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'Annex III, Area 4 — Employment, Workers Management, and Self-Employment, sub-point (a): recruitment and selection, specifically application filtering/screening and candidate evaluation and selection' and concludes 'High-risk, Annex III Area 4(a)'.

expectations.text: States the compliance deadline for Annex III standalone high-risk systems as 2 December 2027 (as amended by the Digital Omnibus, adopted June 2026; originally 2 August 2026). Any deadline stated must be 2 Dec 2027, not Aug 2026.
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'Under the Digital Omnibus (adopted 29 June 2026)... Annex III high-risk obligations apply from 2 December 2027' and notes it is 'later than the originally-legislated 2 August 2026 date'.

expectations.text: Walks through core provider obligations citing Arts. 9-17 (risk management, data governance, technical documentation, logging, transparency to deployers, human oversight, accuracy/robustness/cybersecurity, QMS)
expectations.evidence: Table in section 5 covers Art. 9 (risk management), Art. 10 (data governance), Art. 11 (technical documentation), Art. 12 (logging), Art. 13 (transparency to deployers), Art. 14 (human oversight), Art. 15 (accuracy/robustness/cybersecurity), Art. 16 (provider checklist), Art. 17 (QMS).

expectations.text: Mentions conformity assessment and registration: self-assessment per Annex VI for Annex III point 4 systems, EU declaration of conformity (Art. 47), CE marking (Art. 48), EU database registration (Art. 49)
expectations.evidence: Table states 'Conformity assessment | Art. 43 | Annex III Area 4 (Points 2–8) is self-assessment only — no notified body required' and 'EU Declaration of Conformity & CE marking | Arts. 47–48' and 'EU AI database registration | Art. 49'.

expectations.text: Notes the Art. 6(3) derogation exists but explains it is narrow and does not apply where the system profiles natural persons / materially influences hiring decisions (profiling is always high-risk)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly runs the 'Art. 6(3) exceptions test' and concludes 'None of the narrow non-high-risk exceptions... plausibly apply' and explains the 'Profiling override (Art. 6(3), final subparagraph)... Profiling is always high-risk regardless of any exception claim.'
